title: Extra Work Offer
prompt: You get offered extra work this week. What do you do?

choices.label: Take it and earn extra
choices.ledger_note: Took extra work for more income.

choices.label: Take only part of it
choices.ledger_note: Took a smaller extra job and stayed balanced.

choices.label: Pass and protect your energy
choices.ledger_note: Passed on extra work to protect your schedule.

title: Monthly Tool or Service Contract
prompt: A monthly contract could help you work, but it comes with a future bill.

choices.label: Take the contract
choices.ledger_note: Accepted a monthly contract to support work.

choices.label: Wait until you have more cash
choices.ledger_note: Waited before adding a monthly contract.

choices.label: Say no and keep things simple
choices.ledger_note: Avoided taking on a new fixed cost.

title: Referral Opportunity
prompt: A happy customer could refer more work if you make a strong impression now.

choices.label: Invest $6 in a polished finish
choices.ledger_note: Spent a little to strengthen your reputation.

choices.label: Do a normal finish
choices.ledger_note: Did solid work without extra spending.

choices.label: Rush it and move on
choices.ledger_note: Rushed the work and risked weaker referrals.

title: Supply Problem
prompt: You are low on something important for work.

choices.label: Restock now for $9
choices.ledger_note: Restocked so you can keep working.

choices.label: Borrow or improvise this week
choices.ledger_note: Borrowed or improvised to avoid spending today.

choices.label: Skip the task and lose the chance
choices.ledger_note: Skipped the chance because you were not ready.

title: Promo Push
prompt: You can spend a little to promote your work this week.

choices.label: Spend $7 to promote
choices.ledger_note: Invested a little in promotion.

choices.label: Use word of mouth only
choices.ledger_note: Relied on word of mouth instead.

choices.label: Do nothing
choices.ledger_note: Skipped promoting this week.

title: Customer Wants a Small Fix
prompt: A customer asks for a small fix before leaving a review.

choices.label: Fix it and protect your reputation
choices.ledger_note: Handled the small fix to protect your reputation.

choices.label: Give a small $4 discount instead
choices.ledger_note: Gave a small discount to keep the customer happy.

choices.label: Decline and move on
choices.ledger_note: Declined the fix and risked goodwill.

title: Rush Order Request
prompt: A customer wants something fast and is willing to pay more.

choices.label: Accept the rush for more pay
choices.ledger_note: Accepted a rush job for more pay.

choices.label: Accept but set clear limits
choices.ledger_note: Accepted the rush job with limits.

choices.label: Decline and keep your schedule sane
choices.ledger_note: Declined the rush to protect your schedule.

title: Equipment Upgrade Offer
prompt: A better tool could help you work faster or better.

choices.label: Buy the upgrade for $18
choices.ledger_note: Bought an upgrade to improve your work.

choices.label: Start saving for it instead
choices.ledger_note: Started saving before buying.

choices.label: Keep your current setup
choices.ledger_note: Kept your current setup for now.

title: Schedule Conflict
prompt: Two responsibilities are fighting for the same time slot.

choices.label: Reschedule the work and keep trust
choices.ledger_note: Rescheduled and communicated well.

choices.label: Try to squeeze both in
choices.ledger_note: Tried to squeeze both in and stretched yourself thin.

choices.label: Cancel the work
choices.ledger_note: Canceled the work and took the hit.

title: Bonus Tip
prompt: Someone gives you a tip or bonus after good work.

choices.label: Put the tip into savings
choices.ledger_note: Saved your extra tip money.

choices.label: Save some and keep some
choices.ledger_note: Split your tip between saving and spending.

choices.label: Spend it all today
choices.ledger_note: Kept the extra tip for spending.
